title: How to add money using netbanking on Kite?

text: You can add money to your Zerodha account using a cheque for free. Your account will be updated once the cheque clears, which can take up to 5 working days. Steps to deposit a cheque Write Zerodha Broking Ltd as the payee on your cheque. Write your Kite user ID on the back of the cheque. Courier the cheque to: Zerodha Customer Support Centre 192A 4th Floor, Kalyani Vista 3rd Main Road, JP Nagar 4th Phase Bengaluru, 560076 If you deposit at an HDFC branch If you deposit the cheque directly at any HDFC branch, create a ticket and attach a scanned copy of the cheque or your debited bank statement that includes your bank account number. You need to provide this to verify that you transferred the amount from your bank account linked with Zerodha, since Zerodha does not receive the sender's bank account details. If the bank asks you to mention Zerodha's account number at the back of the cheque, you can write: 57500000302010. Things to keep in mind You will be charged up to ₹350 + 18% GST if your cheque bounces Zerodha will only accept money transferred from your linked bank accounts. You can link more than one bank account with Zerodha. To see your linked bank accounts, visit console.zerodha.com/account/bank You can transfer money instantly to your Zerodha account using UPI, payment gateway, and IMPS/NEFT/RTGS.
title: How to add money to Zerodha using a cheque?

text: You can transfer money to your Zerodha account by adding Zerodha as a beneficiary in your internet banking portal and transferring funds using IMPS, NEFT, or RTGS. All transfers from your linked bank accounts are free at Zerodha, though your bank may charge fees. Bank account details for fund transfers Use these details when adding Zerodha as a beneficiary: Field Details Bank name HDFC Bank Account title ZERODHA BROKING LTD Account number ZERNSE Account type Current account Bank branch Sandoz Branch, Mumbai IFSC HDFC0000240 Transfer methods and timelines Method Time taken Charges IMPS Within 10 minutes Free at Zerodha, bank charges may apply NEFT/RTGS Within 2 hours Free at Zerodha, bank charges may apply You can add Zerodha as a beneficiary for fund transfers using linked bank accounts. If you cannot add the beneficiary or do not see the funds in your Zerodha account within the specified timelines, create a ticket for assistance. Things to keep in mind You can only transfer funds from bank accounts linked to your Zerodha account. You can have multiple bank accounts linked to your account. To view your linked bank accounts, visit console.zerodha.com/account/bank Only fund transfers for more than ₹1 will be updated on the terminal If you have a joint bank account linked to multiple Zerodha accounts, you can only add money using UPI or the payment gateway. Money transferred using IMPS, NEFT, or RTGS will be reversed back to your bank account Do not transfer funds to Zerodha's Yes Bank payout account ending in ******0071, as these transactions must be manually reversed to your account. Only use the bank account details mentioned above. If you have already transferred funds to the Yes Bank account, send a soft copy of your bank statement showing the debit transaction by creating a ticket If you
title: How to transfer money to your Zerodha account using IMPS, NEFT, or RTGS?

text: Only use the bank account details mentioned above. If you have already transferred funds to the Yes Bank account, send a soft copy of your bank statement showing the debit transaction by creating a ticket If you have an NRI PIS account, do not transfer funds using IMPS/NEFT/RTGS. To learn more, see What is the trading process for NRI accounts? If you transfer funds to your trading account between 12 AM and 7:30 AM, it won't reflect on the trading terminal until after 7:30 AM.
title: How to transfer money to your Zerodha account using IMPS, NEFT, or RTGS?

text: You can transfer funds to your Zerodha account in three different ways: UPI: Transfer funds instantly from your registered bank account using UPI for free. You must initiate UPI transfers from Zerodha's payment gateway for the funds to be updated in your Zerodha account instantly. Netbanking: You can use the Netbanking facility to transfer funds to your Zerodha account. However, there are charges of ₹9 + 18% GST. IMPS/NEFT/RTGS/Cheque: You can transfer funds using IMPS/NEFT/RTGS for free at Zerodha. However, your bank may apply charges. You can also transfer funds using a cheque for free. Things to keep in mind Zerodha accepts UPI transfers made only through the Add Funds interface on Kite. Digital wallet transfers or transfers initiated directly from a UPI app will not be accepted. If you receive an email or phone call claiming to be from Zerodha soliciting fund transfers to an account number that is not mentioned on the Funds page or if you notice any spoofed (duplicate/unofficial) Zerodha website, create a ticket immediately with those details. Never provide sensitive account information like support code, password, client ID or personal details in response to an email. SEBI regulations do not allow Zerodha or any other broker to accept deposits via cash/Demand Draft (DD).
title: What are the different ways to transfer money to your Zerodha account?

text: You can add money to your Zerodha account through the Kite app even if your UPI app is installed on a different device. You will need to validate the transfer on the device where your UPI app is installed. Steps to add money Tap on Add funds. Enter the amount you want to transfer and tap on Netbanking. Tap on UPI and enter your UPI ID. Validate the transfer on your UPI app on the other device. Things to keep in mind You can make up to 35 UPI transfers per day, with each transfer up to ₹5 lakh. Your bank may impose additional restrictions on the amount per transaction. If you transfer funds between 12 AM and 7:30 AM, they will not reflect in your Zerodha account until after 7:30 AM. You can only transfer funds from a UPI ID linked to your bank account registered with Zerodha.
title: How to add money on Kite app if your UPI app is on a different device?

text: No, you cannot transfer funds using a digital wallet. Regulations require you to transfer funds only from your verified bank accounts. However, you can transfer funds using UPI.
title: Can I transfer funds using a digital wallet?

text: No, you cannot transfer funds to your Zerodha account using a debit or credit card. You also cannot use credit cards for any saving and investing products, such as fixed deposits, investments in stock markets etc. This applies to all brokers. Regulations mandate that you transfer funds through the trading app and other approved payment methods. You can transfer money to your Zerodha account using UPI, payment gateway, IMPS/NEFT/RTGS, or through cheque.
title: Can I transfer funds to Zerodha account using debit or credit card?

text: No, you cannot transfer funds using cash or demand draft. Regulations mandate that you transfer funds through the trading app and other approved payment methods, which applies to all brokers.
title: Can I transfer funds to my Zerodha account using cash or demand draft?

text: As per the SEBI mandate, funds can be transferred only from bank accounts linked to the Zerodha account. Any funds transferred through IMPS, NEFT, RTGS, or cheque from an unlinked bank account are refunded to the source bank account within 24-48 working hours. If the reversal does not occur within this timeframe, please create a ticket and attach a bank statement showing the transaction details. Multiple bank accounts can be linked to a Zerodha account, i.e., one primary bank account and two secondary bank accounts. Did you know? An Overdraft (OD) bank account cannot be linked with Zerodha.
title: Can funds be transferred using IMPS, NEFT, RTGS or cheque from bank accounts not linked to the Zerodha account?

text: Yes, clients can use funds from the equity segment to trade in the commodities segment through the single ledger facility. To use this facility, clients must activate the commodity segment from Console.
title: Can funds in the equity segment be used to trade in commodities?

text: You might receive a wrong UPI collect request from Zerodha in the following scenarios: Another Zerodha client entered your UPI ID by mistake This happens when another client mistakenly enters your UPI ID while adding funds to their Kite account. Example scenario: Two Zerodha account holders: Ashwin K (UPI ID: ashwin.k@okaxis) and Ashwin L (UPI ID: ashwini.l@okaxis) If Ashwin L enters ashwin.k@okaxis instead of their own UPI ID, you (Ashwin K) will receive the wrong request What you should do: Decline the transaction on your UPI app if you did not initiate the transfer. Even if you approve the transaction by mistake, the fund transfer will fail because your bank account is not mapped to their Zerodha account. Zerodha only accepts fund transfers from linked bank accounts. You can check your linked bank accounts by visiting console.zerodha.com/account/bank. Someone has access to your Zerodha account If someone has unauthorised access to your account, they might place requests for you to add funds. This could happen if: You shared your Zerodha login details with someone You fell victim to a phishing attack where fraudsters tricked you into adding funds to your account What you should do: Immediately reset your Kite login credentials. Learn more about phishing attacks by visiting zerodha.com/z-connect/phishing-scam. The fraudster's plan is typically to execute unauthorised transactions with the transferred funds. Learn about common stock market scams by visiting zerodha.com/z-connect/stock-market-scams. A fraudster registered Zerodha as their name in a UPI app Scammers sometimes register "Zerodha" as their display name in UPI apps to trick users into transferring money. Zerodha's official UPI IDs When adding funds to Kite, you will only receive requests from these official UPI IDs: zerodhabroking@hdfcbank zerodha.broking@icici zerodhabroking@axisbank zerodhabroking@yesbank zerodhabroking.brk@validhdfc zerodhabroking.brk@validicici zerodhabroking.brk@validaxis zerodhabrokingsignup@hdfcbank (when you open your account with Zerodha) What you should do: If you receive requests
title: Why have I received a UPI collect request from Zerodha, which I did not raise?

text: adding funds to Kite, you will only receive requests from these official UPI IDs: zerodhabroking@hdfcbank zerodha.broking@icici zerodhabroking@axisbank zerodhabroking@yesbank zerodhabroking.brk@validhdfc zerodhabroking.brk@validicici zerodhabroking.brk@validaxis zerodhabrokingsignup@hdfcbank (when you open your account with Zerodha) What you should do: If you receive requests from any other UPI ID claiming to be Zerodha, email us immediately at [email protected] or create a ticket.
title: Why have I received a UPI collect request from Zerodha, which I did not raise?

text: Funds transferred via IMPS, NEFT or RTGS may be refunded to the source bank account for several reasons: Unlinked bank account: If a transfer is made from a bank account not linked to the trading account, the funds are automatically refunded. To learn more, see Can funds be transferred using IMPS, NEFT, RTGS or cheque from bank accounts not linked to the Zerodha account? Incorrect details: If the bank account number or IFSC code is incorrect or outdated in the Zerodha account, the funds will be refunded. This may happen if the bank has changed its branch details. To learn more, see What to do if the bank has changed the branch IFSC, MICR or account number? Joint bank account: If funds are transferred via IMPS, NEFT or RTGS from a joint bank account linked to multiple Zerodha accounts, they will be refunded. To learn more, see Can joint bank accounts be linked with a Zerodha account? Inactive account: If the trading account is inactive or has not yet been activated after opening, the funds may be refunded. To learn more see, How long does it take to open an account, and where can the account opening status be tracked? Bank settlement account: If the funds are transferred from a bank's settlement account number, they will not be added to the Zerodha account and will be refunded. In such cases, use other available fund transfer methods.
title: Why are funds added through IMPS, NEFT or RTGS getting refunded to the source bank account?

text: You can add funds using net banking, UPI, NEFT, RTGS, IMPS, and cheque. You can withdraw any amount up to your available withdrawable balance. Fund transfer limits The fund transfer limits for various modes of payment per day are as follows: Net banking on Kite: You can make up to 25 net banking transfers per day. Zerodha does not impose any limits on transfer amounts. However, your bank may have restrictions on transfer amounts. UPI: You can make up to 35 UPI transfers per day, with each transfer up to ₹5 lakh. Your bank may impose additional restrictions on the amount per transaction. NEFT and RTGS: Zerodha does not impose any limits on NEFT and RTGS transfers. However, your bank may have restrictions on transfer amounts. IMPS: Most banks allow IMPS transfers up to ₹2 lakh per transaction. Zerodha does not impose any limits, but your bank may have restrictions on the amount per transaction. Withdrawal limits You can withdraw any amount (minimum ₹1) subject to the availability of withdrawable balance in your trading account. For withdrawals up to ₹5 crores, you can place a request directly from Console. However, if you wish to withdraw more than ₹5 crores, please create a ticket. For instant withdrawals, the withdrawal requests must be for a minimum of ₹100 and not exceed ₹2,00,000 per day.
title: What are the limits on fund transfers and fund withdrawals in Zerodha?

text: Zerodha uses NTT DATA payment services (ATOM) and Pine labs payment gateway (Billdesk) as its payment gateway vendors. When funds are transferred to Zerodha accounts through net banking, clients are redirected to them. However, if funds are transferred through an IDFC 3-in-1 account, ICICI, SBI, HDFC, Kotak or Axis bank, clients will not be redirected to NTT or Pine labs as Zerodha has a direct payment gateway with these banks. To learn how to transfer funds using net banking, see How to add funds using netbanking on Kite?
title: What are the names of the payment gateway vendors in Zerodha?

text: Sometimes, banks undergo internal restructuring, get merged or acquired, due to which the IFSC, MICR and account numbers can change. In such cases, the account details must be updated with Zerodha. The details can be updated by updating the bank account online. If the primary bank account details are changed, see How to change the primary bank account? to update the primary bank account details. If the secondary bank account details are changed, remove the existing secondary bank account and relink the bank account with the updated details. See How to remove a secondary bank account linked to a Zerodha account? and How to add a secondary bank account to a Zerodha account? to learn more. The online modification is possible only if the mobile number is linked to an Aadhaar. If the Aadhaar is unlinked or if a non-individual account (HUF, Corporate, or Partnership) is held, follow the offline process explained below. Download the account modification form (PDF), and send it to Zerodha's head office along with the following bank account proof: Personalised cancelled cheque with the account holder's name printed on the cheque leaf. Self-attested bank statement with IFSC and MICR clearly visible. Bank passbook. Courier the forms to: Zerodha Customer Support Centre 192A 4th Floor, Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road, JP Nagar 4th Phase, Bengaluru, 560076 Did you know? The bank account must be in the name of the Zerodha account holder. A charge of ₹25 + 18% GST will be levied for primary bank account modification.
title: What to do if the bank has changed the branch IFSC, MICR or account number?

text: Funds transferred through IMPS usually take up to 10 minutes to reflect in the Zerodha account after transferring during market hours. Fund transfers only from linked bank accounts are accepted. If funds are transferred from an unlinked bank account, see Can funds be transferred using IMPS, NEFT, RTGS or cheque from bank accounts not linked to the Zerodha account? to learn how to reverse such transfers.
title: Why are the funds transferred through IMPS not reflecting in the Zerodha account?

text: You receive email and SMS notifications when Zerodha transfers funds back to your primary bank account due to quarterly settlements, even though you didn't request a withdrawal. This is an automatic process that occurs as part of regulatory requirements.
title: Why did I receive a message saying my withdrawal request was processed when I never made one?

text: When you pledge securities in your demat account to trade F&O, your withdrawable balance changes based on the collateral margin blocked for your F&O positions and the cash in your account. At least 50% of the margins blocked for F&O positions have to come in the form of cash or cash equivalent collateral. The other 50% of the margin requirement can come in the form of other non-cash equivalent collateral margins. Example scenario You initiate an F&O position requiring ₹1,00,000 margin. Before opening this position, you have: Cash: ₹70,000 Collateral margin: ₹80,000 from pledged securities (after applying required haircut) Cash equivalent: ₹20,000 from pledged liquidbees Here's how the system allocates your ₹1,00,000 F&O margin: Component Available for use (INR) Utilised (INR) Balance (INR) Collateral Margin 80,000 50,000 30,000 Cash Equivalent 20,000 20,000 0.00 Cash 70,000 30,000 40,000 Total Margin collected 1,00,000 This calculation leaves you with a withdrawable balance of ₹40,000. Withdrawable balance formula The system calculates your withdrawable balance using this formula: Withdrawable balance = (Available cash balance - Proceeds from stocks sold today - F&O profits today - Funds added today) Where: Unsettled credits = Today's credits in equity segment + Today's credits in the derivative segment Collateral utilised = Total collateral used towards margin requirements from cash and non-cash equivalents If you don't have enough collateral margins to cover 50% of the blocked margin, the system charges the difference from your available cash balance, which further reduces your withdrawable balance. Things to keep in mind Collateral margin from pledged stocks or liquid mutual funds for F&O trading does not count as part of your withdrawable balance. Funds you add during the day become available for trading immediately. However, you can only withdraw these funds after one day due to end-of-day balance reconciliation requirements. You can track securities
title: How is withdrawable balance calculated after pledging?

text: as part of your withdrawable balance. Funds you add during the day become available for trading immediately. However, you can only withdraw these funds after one day due to end-of-day balance reconciliation requirements. You can track securities that form part of cash and non-cash components on the approved list by visiting zerodha.com/approved-securities.
title: How is withdrawable balance calculated after pledging?

text: Yes, you can withdraw money to any bank account linked with Zerodha by selecting the preferred bank when withdrawing funds. However, the bank account must be penny drop verified to process the withdrawal.
title: Can I withdraw money to my secondary bank account?

text: If you have closed your positions today, you cannot withdraw the funds immediately since exchanges in India follow a T+1 rolling settlement cycle. Same-day withdrawal conditions If you have not traded during the day, have no open positions, have not placed an instant withdrawal request and have sufficient balance on the day of withdrawal, Zerodha will process your withdrawal request on the same day. If you place your request before 5 PM, your money will be credited to your bank account on the same day. However, if you place your request after 5 PM, your money will be credited on the next day. Example scenario You sell shares on Monday. The funds from your sale are credited the next day, i.e. Tuesday. If you execute no additional trades and submit a fund withdrawal request, Zerodha will credit the funds by the end of the same day, i.e. Tuesday.
title: Why are the sales proceeds not withdrawable on the same day?

text: You can place a withdrawal request by following these steps: Kite app Tap on user ID. Tap on Funds. Tap on Withdraw. Enter the amount to be withdrawn, tap on Continue. If multiple bank accounts are linked, select your preferred bank account. Tap on Confirm. Kite web Log in to Kite. Click on Funds. Click on Withdraw. Enter the amount to be withdrawn and click on Continue If multiple bank accounts are linked, select your preferred bank account. Click on Confirm. Console Log in to Console. Click on Funds. Click on Withdrawal. Enter the amount to be withdrawn and click on Continue If multiple bank accounts are linked, select your preferred bank account. Click on Confirm. Things to keep in mind You can only withdraw the amount shown as your withdrawable balance. You cannot withdraw money you added to your account on the same day. Your withdrawal may be rejected, or you may receive a lower amount if your balance reduces after the trade settlement process at the end of the day. If you have not traded during the day, have no open positions, have not placed an instant withdrawal request and have a sufficient balance on the withdrawal day, your withdrawal request will be processed on the same day. For same-day withdrawals, money will be credited to your bank account on the same day if you place the request before 5 PM. If you place the request after 5 PM, you will receive the money the next day. If you have placed CNC sell orders, your regular withdrawal request will only be processed at 10:00 PM. You can also withdraw money instantly from your Zerodha account using the instant withdrawal feature. For detailed information about withdrawal processing times, visit How much time does it take to process a withdrawal
title: How to withdraw money from the Zerodha account?

text: processed at 10:00 PM. You can also withdraw money instantly from your Zerodha account using the instant withdrawal feature. For detailed information about withdrawal processing times, visit How much time does it take to process a withdrawal request?
title: How to withdraw money from the Zerodha account?

text: If you encounter the Instant Payouts window is closed error before 9 AM, or if this error persists after 9 AM, close the Console withdrawal page and retry after 15 minutes. Zerodha's instant withdrawal feature allows you to withdraw money from your Zerodha account to your linked bank account immediately, with no charges. Requirements for instant withdrawal To use instant withdrawal, you must meet these conditions: Submit your request only once per day between 9:00 AM and 4:00 PM on all days, including weekends Withdraw a minimum of ₹100 and maximum of ₹2,00,000 per day No pending or executed orders other than Longterm (CNC) sell or MTF sell orders, and no open positions other than overnight positions How to place an instant withdrawal request Tap on your user ID. Tap on Funds. Tap on Instant. Enter the amount. (minimum ₹100, maximum ₹2,00,000). Tap on Continue and then on Confirm. Things to keep in mind You cannot use instant withdrawal for money you added during the same day Credit from the previous day's sale of holdings will not be included in your withdrawable balance on settlement, and trading holidays, and weekends. For example, credit from holdings sold on 17/05/2024 (Friday) will be available for withdrawal only on 21/05/2024 (Tuesday), since 20/05/2024 (Monday) is a settlement holiday If your instant withdrawal fails, you must place a regular withdrawal request, as instant withdrawals are only allowed once per day Your withdrawal amount must be a whole number without any decimal value Your withdrawable balance on Console will not update immediately after you withdraw money using instant withdrawal If you have linked an Orbis account to Zerodha, you cannot use the instant withdrawal feature. You can use instant withdrawal even if you have open Good Till Triggered (GTT) orders.
title: How to withdraw money instantly in Zerodha, and how does it work?

text: If you have an Orbis account linked to Zerodha, you cannot place fund withdrawal requests on Console. To place a fund withdrawal request for your Orbis account, fill out this form (DOC), and the funds will be credited to your primary bank account. Processing timeline If you submit your withdrawal request before 6 PM, you will receive the funds on the next working day If you submit your withdrawal request after 6 PM or during the weekend, you will receive the funds after two working days Example scenario You place a withdrawal request on Wednesday at 7:00 PM. Zerodha will process your request on Thursday. The funds are credited on Friday to your primary bank account linked to Zerodha. Things to keep in mind If you hold an Orbis account, place a regular withdrawal request on Kite for the MCX segment Zerodha will process withdrawal requests you place on public holidays on the next trading day
title: How to place a fund withdrawal request for Orbis account?

text: When you withdraw funds, you can choose which bank account you want to receive the money: your primary or secondary bank accounts. Zerodha only pays out to bank accounts verified through a penny drop, which confirms that the account belongs to you. What is a penny drop? A penny drop is a verification process where Zerodha credits a nominal amount to your bank account to confirm that the account is valid and belongs to you. Only penny-drop-verified accounts are available for withdrawal. If your bank account is not yet penny-drop-verified, you will see Account verification is pending. You will not be able to withdraw to this account until verification is complete. To verify your bank account: Visit console.zerodha.com/account/bank. Tap or click on the pencil icon. Enter your bank account number and IFSC code, and accept the terms and conditions. Select whether you want to receive the OTP via email or mobile, and click on Send OTP. Enter the OTP and click on Verify. Once verified, the account becomes available for withdrawal. There are no charges for penny-drop verification. How to withdraw to a verified bank account Open Kite app or Kite web and navigate to funds section. Tap or click on Withdraw. Enter the amount and select the bank account you want to withdraw to: primary or secondary bank accounts. Tap or click on Confirm. Why is a bank account not available for withdrawal? A bank account may be unavailable for withdrawal if: The penny drop verification has not been completed. The bank account details on Console are outdated or incorrect.
title: How to select a bank account for withdrawal in Zerodha?

text: Your payout amount could be lower than your requested payout because your withdrawable balance can reduce after the end of day process when the charges and net obligation (any trading losses) for the day are debited from your account. The end of day process typically happens between 5 PM and 9 PM and is dependent on the time exchanges share the end of day files. How withdrawal requests are processed If you place a withdrawal request for an amount higher than your withdrawable balance, Zerodha will credit the available withdrawable balance to your bank account. Example scenario You place a withdrawal request for ₹12,000 at 10:00 AM After the end of day processes, if your withdrawable amount is ₹9,000, Zerodha will credit this amount to your bank account While the funds you deposit to your trading account during the day are available for trading immediately, you can only withdraw them the next day, owing to the end-of-day settlement and reconciliation processes, because of which your payout amount could be lower.
title: Why is the payout amount less than what was requested?

text: Withdrawable balance is the amount you can transfer from your Zerodha account to your primary or secondary bank account. This balance may differ from the net available funds shown in your funds statement because exchanges don't settle funds from equity and F&O trades instantly. Exchanges require T+1 day to settle funds from selling equities and F&O trades to your Zerodha account before you can withdraw them. The withdrawable balance may change after market hours if you have traded during the day. This happens because Zerodha updates all charges and obligations after the end-of-day process between 5 PM and 9 PM. Pledging stocks can also affect your withdrawable balance. While you can use funds deposited to your trading account immediately for trading, you can only withdraw them the next day due to end-of-day settlement and reconciliation processes.
title: What is the withdrawable balance?

text: You can invest your withdrawable balance directly into Zerodha's LIQUIDCASE ETF instead of withdrawing it to your bank account. This allows you to park your funds in a liquid security with relatively low risk and potentially better returns than keeping them in a savings bank account. Zerodha Nifty 1D Rate Liquid ETF is Zerodha Fund House's Liquidcase that invests in low-risk overnight instruments like Treasury Bills and Repos. The ETF focuses on Net Asset Value (NAV) growth rather than dividend distribution. You can sell LIQUIDCASE units anytime through Kite, as they trade like regular stocks on the exchange. To invest your withdrawable balance in LIQUIDCASE ETF from Kite app: Tap on your user ID. Tap on Funds. Tap on Withdraw. Tap on Invest in Zerodha AMC LIQUIDCASE and enter the amount to invest. Tap on Continue to place the order. You can also invest your withdrawable balance in Zerodha AMC Liquidcase by visiting console.zerodha.com/funds/overview. Post-market and weekend orders You can also place LIQUIDCASE investment orders after market hours. All orders placed after 3:30 PM, on trading holidays, and on weekends are collected and placed on Kite on the next trading day. Things to keep in mind You must enter an amount above the Last Traded Price (LTP) of the ETF to purchase at least 1 unit. Zerodha calculates the maximum units you can purchase based on the ETF's LTP and the amount you enter. Investing in LIQUIDCASE is treated as a regular investment. This means that regular charges for Liquid ETFs will be debited from your equity account on the trading day. The same charges apply when you sell. To learn more about Zerodha’s Liquid case, visit Zerodha Nifty 1D Rate Liquid ETF.
title: How to park your withdrawable balance in LIQUIDCASE ETF?

text: The withdrawable balance will be negative in the following scenarios: Your Zerodha account is in a debit balance. Your funds are yet to be settled to your Zerodha account. In India, exchanges follow a rolling settlement cycle. Exchanges require T+1 day to settle funds from selling equities and F&O trades to your Zerodha account before you can withdraw them. Until then, the withdrawal amount is displayed in negative, indicating that it is still being processed for settlement. Your withdrawable balance becomes positive once the funds are settled. Withdrawal requests placed when the withdrawal balance is negative will not be processed.
title: Why is the withdrawable balance negative?

text: Your withdrawal request can be rejected for these reasons: Unsettled trade funds: You cannot withdraw funds from trades that are yet to be settled. Exchanges require T+1 day to settle funds from selling equities and F&O trades before you can withdraw them. For intraday and F&O trades, this includes realised profits, mark-to-market (M2M), or the sell value of options. Same-day deposits: You can trade with money added during the day immediately, but you cannot withdraw it on the same day. You can withdraw these funds after one day.1 Payment gateway delays: Money added through the payment gateway can take up to T+1 days to be credited to Zerodha. When it is not credited immediately, Zerodha uses its own capital to allow you to trade on T day, making same-day withdrawals not possible.2 What happens when you request more than your available balance If you place a withdrawal request for an amount greater than your withdrawal balance, Zerodha will credit only the available withdrawable balance to your bank account. Example scenario Your withdrawable balance is ₹9000 You place a withdrawal request for ₹12,000 Zerodha will credit ₹9000 to your bank account Notes 1Zerodha processes withdrawal requests after the trading session, since it must consider obligations for all trades during the day. Due to potential issues with crediting funds, Zerodha processes withdrawal requests only after reconciling funds with respective banks. Banks share transaction details by early morning of the next day (T+1 day) due to the volume of transactions. After receiving transaction details, Zerodha reconciles them and processes fund withdrawals. In some cases, Zerodha has received incorrect confirmation of fund credits from banks, and transactions have been reversed. To avoid such issues, Zerodha processes fund withdrawal requests on T+1 day. 2When you transfer funds through payment gateways, you receive credit on the
title: Why was my withdrawal request rejected?

text: received incorrect confirmation of fund credits from banks, and transactions have been reversed. To avoid such issues, Zerodha processes fund withdrawal requests on T+1 day. 2When you transfer funds through payment gateways, you receive credit on the same day. However, payment gateways settle the funds to Zerodha's account only on T+1 or T+2 days. Until then, Zerodha funds the margins for your trades from its own capital. Since Zerodha receives credit of funds transferred through payment gateways on T+1 or T+2 days, processing withdrawal requests of funds added on the same day would require funding them from Zerodha's own capital. This is why Zerodha cannot process withdrawal requests for funds added on the same day.
title: Why was my withdrawal request rejected?

text: You can instantly withdraw money from your Zerodha account using the Instant withdrawal feature. Zerodha processes fund withdrawal requests at specific cut-off times. If you place your request before the cut-off time, your funds are typically credited to your bank account within 24 hours of the cut-off time. If you place your request after the cut-off time, it takes an additional working day (up to 48 hours in total) for your funds to be credited. The withdrawal cut-off time for your account depends on whether your Commodity segment is activated. If your Commodity segment is not activated Weekdays (Mon–Fri): 10:00 PM Saturday: 4:30 PM Sunday & Public holidays: Requests are processed on the next working day If your Commodity segment is activated Weekdays (Mon–Fri): 11:59 PM Saturday: 4:30 PM Sunday & Public Holidays: Requests are processed on the next working day Create a ticket if you have not received your funds after the expected timeframe. Things to keep in mind The Recent withdrawals section includes a reference number for Equity payouts. If you have not traded during the day, have no open positions, have not placed an instant withdrawal request, and have a sufficient balance on the withdrawal day, your withdrawal request will be processed on the same day. If you place your request before 5:00 PM, your money will be credited to your bank account on the same day. If you place your request after 5:00 PM, your money will be credited on the next day. If you have placed CNC sell orders, your regular withdrawal request will only be processed at 10:00 PM. If you have enabled single ledger, your fund withdrawal requests are processed only at 11:59 PM, irrespective of the segments.
title: How much time does it take to process a withdrawal request?

text: You can withdraw funds from selling shares or closing positions after T+1 day, once the trades are settled and appear in your withdrawable balance. The settlement cycle for all instruments traded on Indian exchanges is T+1 day, where T stands for the trading day. This means you cannot withdraw the proceeds immediately after selling shares or closing F&O positions. If you sell shares from your holdings or close F&O positions on Friday, you can withdraw the proceeds on Monday morning.
title: When can I withdraw funds after selling shares or closing positions?

text: For stock SIPs, your funds are deducted from your Zerodha account. Your SIP order will fail if you do not have sufficient funds available on the specified date. You can use the eMandate facility to ensure your Zerodha account has the necessary funds.
title: Are the funds for stock SIP deducted from the bank account?

text: You can find the complete list of banks supporting eMandates here (WEB). Banks with the Netbanking status as Live support eMandates. The following banks are temporarily disabled from creating mandates: Central Bank. Standard Chartered. Indian Overseas Bank (IOB).
title: Which banks support eMandates?

text: You can stop auto-debit of funds by cancelling the schedule instead of cancelling the entire eMandate. This allows you to use the same eMandate to set up schedules in the future without creating a new eMandate. Cancel a schedule You must cancel schedules at least 3 working days in advance from the next account credit date (4 working days for SBI accounts). If you cancel a schedule after an upcoming debit is confirmed, the funds will be debited from your bank account and added to your Zerodha account. However, all future debits for that schedule will be cancelled. You can withdraw these funds from your Zerodha account if required. Cancel the entire eMandate An eMandate can also be completely cancelled. To cancel, visit console.zerodha.com/funds/mandates and follow the below steps: Click on Delete mandate. Confirm the request. 3. Mandates that are under process show up with the Pending Cancellation status. Your eMandate takes up to 5 working days to be deleted. You can create a new mandate once the old one is deleted.
title: How to cancel an eMandate?

text: This happens when your browser blocks pop-ups by default. You can create eMandates after enabling pop-ups by clicking on Always show in your iOS web browser.
title: Why is there a difficulty in setting up eMandate on iOS devices?

text: You should schedule your eMandate transaction a few days before your stock SIP date because the bank confirmation of the eMandate transfer can be delayed. For example, if your stock SIP is scheduled for the 5th of every month, you should schedule your eMandate transaction for the 2nd or 3rd. Your bank account is debited one working day before the eMandate scheduled date, and you receive SMS and email confirmations one day in advance.
title: When should the eMandate transaction be scheduled to ensure sufficient funds for stock SIP?

text: Some banks do not allow you to create eMandates from joint bank accounts. You can create standing instructions instead by adding Zerodha as a beneficiary using Zerodha's bank account details to automatically transfer funds on predefined dates from your netbanking portal. Here is the process to create a standing instruction in HDFC Bank as an example: Select Transfer within the bank. Under Transfer type, select Set Standing Instruction. Select Start Date, Frequency & End Date as per your requirement. Select Zerodha as Beneficiary and Continue.
title: Can eMandate be set up using a joint account?

text: You can schedule fund transfers once you have created an eMandate. To schedule fund transfers, follow these steps: Visit console.zerodha.com/funds/mandates. Specify the Tag name, Account credit date, Frequency, and the Amount under Set new schedule. If your eMandate transaction fails due to insufficient funds, the fund transfer will not be retried. You will need to transfer funds manually to your Zerodha account. The maximum limit per schedule is ₹1 lakh. Multiple schedules can be created in a single day, up to a cumulative limit of ₹1 crore.
title: How to schedule an eMandate transaction?

text: You can use the eMandate facility to automatically transfer up to ₹1 crore at pre-set schedules from your bank account to your Zerodha account. You cannot use current bank accounts to create eMandates. Zerodha does not charge for eMandate registrations or transactions. However, if a transaction fails due to insufficient funds, your bank may charge a penalty. Creating an eMandate Follow these steps to create an eMandate: Visit console.zerodha.com/funds/mandates and click on Create a new mandate. Select the account for which the mandate is to be created. Authenticate the bank account using a debit card or netbanking. Follow the on-screen instructions to complete the process on the bank’s mandate approval gateway. Once the mandate is registered, create a schedule to transfer funds to the Zerodha account. It may take up to 5 days for the bank to activate the mandate. Creating a schedule Follow these steps to create a schedule: Name the schedule. Select fund transfer date. Select frequency and the amount to be transferred. Click on Create schedule.
title: How to set up automatic fund transfers from a bank account to a Zerodha trading account?

text: There are no charges for eMandate registrations or transaction costs for transferring funds using eMandate at Zerodha. However, your bank may charge you a penalty if your transaction fails due to insufficient funds. Check with the bank for the latest charges. The table below shows the bank penalty charges for eMandate failure due to insufficient funds: Bank eMandate failure charge HDFC Bank ₹450 for the 1st instance, ₹500 for the 2nd, and ₹550 for subsequent instances within a month. Senior citizens receive a ₹50 discount per instance. ICICI Bank ₹500 per instance. State Bank of India ₹250 per instance. Axis Bank ₹500 per instance. Kotak Mahindra Bank ₹500 per instance. Punjab National Bank ₹250 per instance. Yes Bank ₹25 per instance. Banks also charge mandate verification charges. title: What are the charges for eMandates?

text: Your e-Mandate registration fails when the authentication using net banking or your debit card is unsuccessful. This happens if you close the authentication window before entering your credentials or if you enter incorrect credentials. Zerodha cannot determine the specific reason for the failure; only your bank has this information. If your mandate creation fails, you can recreate it from Console. Banks do not support e-Mandates for customers using current, joint, and NRE-PIS bank accounts. If you cannot set up your e-Mandate successfully on Console, you can place standing instructions to automatically transfer money through your bank's net banking portal by adding Zerodha's account as a beneficiary.
title: Why did my e-Mandate registration fail?

text: You can transfer up to ₹1 crore per day through eNACH mandates. If you need to transfer more than ₹1 crore in a single day, you can transfer manually using other available methods. Setting up scheduled transfers Once you receive approval for your mandate, you can create a schedule that specifies the exact amount and date for debiting money from your account. You can create multiple schedules as long as your total daily debits do not exceed the maximum limit of ₹1 crore.
title: What is the daily limit for eMandate transactions?

text: Your bank debits the money before the scheduled eMandate transaction date to ensure the funds are available in your trading account on the specified date. Why this happens eMandate transfers take one working day to reflect in your trading account. Your bank debits the money during banking hours, but the National Payments Corporation of India (NPCI) credits Zerodha's bank accounts and confirms the transaction at the end of the day. Your bank therefore debits the funds at least one working day in advance so the money appears in your trading account on the transaction date.
title: Why does money get debited from my bank account before the scheduled eMandate transaction day?

text: Your eMandate registration typically completes within 5 working days. However, sometimes Zerodha does not receive confirmation from the bank on the registration, and the mandate remains in a pending status on Console. Since your bank must provide the confirmation, Zerodha cannot provide a timeline for successful verification. However, Zerodha periodically follows up with the respective banks through its channels in such cases.
title: Why is my eMandate registration pending for more than 5 working days?

text: The email ID and mobile number registered with the AMC are the same as those registered with Zerodha. These details are also captured by Central Depository Services (India) Limited (CDSL), the depository, and shared with the Registrar and Transfer agents (RTA). You can change your email ID or mobile number registered with the AMC by updating these details with Zerodha. Things to keep in mind The RTAs update their database every weekend. Your Coin investments are held in your demat account, so the nominee(s) added to your Zerodha account also apply to your Coin investments.
title: How to change email ID or mobile number registered with the AMC for Coin investments?

text: Your investments made via Coin cannot be seen on the AMC website for these reasons: Your investments made on Coin are in demat mode, and the AMC website only displays investments made in the folio mode Your mobile number or email is not updated with the RTA, which prevents you from logging in
title: Why are my investments made on Coin not visible on the AMC website?

text: You cannot directly switch between two different schemes of the same AMC on Coin. However, you can redeem one fund and purchase another fund within the same AMC. Even though the funds are not credited to your bank account, capital gains tax is still applicable.
title: Can I switch between two different schemes of the same AMC on Coin?

text: Your mutual fund NAV displayed on Console reflects the T-2 day value because the NAV announcement timings vary depending on the type of scheme. NAV announcement timings by scheme type Equity scheme 9 PM on trading day (T day) Liquid scheme 11 PM on T day International underlying scheme 1:30 PM on T+1 day Here's how the NAV data reaches Console: Data vendors receive the data from AMFI and update it on T+1 day in the morning. The Bhav copy for mutual funds is generated by T+1 day at 3 PM. Console uses this Bhav copy to calculate your mutual fund P&L on the night of T+1 day. On T+2 day, your holdings on Console reflect the NAV as of T day and provide an accurate representation of your holdings. You can track your holding data for mutual funds as per T-1 day's NAV on Coin.
title: Why does the mutual fund NAV display T-2 day value on Console?

text: The current mutual fund value on Console is based on the T-2 days NAV for the following reasons: For most Mutual Fund schemes, the AMC officially declares the NAV (Net Asset Value) at the end of the trading day, usually around 11 PM. This information is then transmitted to AMFI (Association of Mutual Funds in India), which updates its data by midnight or sometimes as late as 3-4 AM on the following day, which we refer to as T+1 day. For different types of schemes: For Equity Schemes, the NAV is announced by 9 PM on the same trading day (T-day). For Liquid Schemes, the NAV is announced by 11 PM on T-day. For schemes with international assets, the NAV is announced by 12 PM on T+1 day. Additionally, for international schemes, a report known as the Bhav copy, containing daily trading data for Mutual Funds, is generated by T+1 day at 3 PM. Console uses this Bhav copy to calculate your Mutual Fund Profit and Loss (P&L) on the evening of T+1 day. As a result, by T+2 day, the information Console displays accurately represents your Mutual Fund's value as of T-day. This approach ensures that you receive the most precise and up to date representation of your holdings. You can track your holdings data for mutual funds as per T-1 days NAV on Coin.
title: Why is the current mutual fund value on Console based on the T-2 days NAV?

text: Console displays your buy average with a discrepant symbol in the following cases: If you transferred mutual funds from another demat account to Zerodha, but the system has not updated the original purchase price. If you dematerialised the mutual funds. You will see a discrepant symbol in the following places: Coin dashboard Under the Holdings section on Coin. On Console How to fix the discrepancy To fix the discrepancy, follow these steps: Visit Console. Click on Portfolio and then Holdings. Click on Options and then on View discrepancy. Click on the fund and then on Add trade. Enter the Purchase date, Avg price, Qty and Type as others. In case of a partial discrepancy, the transactions that are already available in the MF trade book will show up under the View transactions option. If a few discrepant entries are added, it is displayed on Coin as shown below: The buy average will be calculated as per the price entered and will be displayed under holdings. How to edit or delete a discrepant entry Follow these steps: Click on Options. To edit, click on Edit trade and make the required changes. To delete, click on Delete trade. The discrepant entry can only be edited or deleted while the status is Pending. Once the recalculation is complete, the trade entry cannot be edited or deleted and the buy average will be permanently set. The average price for the holdings will be calculated based on the First In First Out (FIFO) principle after considering the discrepant entry. The buy average is updated within 24 working hours after the discrepant entry is made. Did you know? Newly allotted units may have the buy average as NA.
title: Why is the buy average for some mutual funds shown as a discrepancy?

text: To fill out the dematerialisation form (PDF), follow these steps: Enter the Date. Ignore the DRF No. field. In the DP ID field, mention Zerodha's DP ID. In the Folio No. field, mention the folio number of the mutual fund as per the statement. In the ISIN field, mention the ISIN number of the mutual fund as per the statement. Under quantity, mention ALL. Under lock-in details (only for ELSS funds), mention the reason as ELSS, and the end of lock-in period date under Expiry date . text: You must stop all ongoing SIPs before initiating rematerialisation. Rematerialisation is the process of converting units from demat mode to physical mode, RTA mode, or offline mode. Documents required You need to submit the following documents: 2 Remat request forms (PDF) signed in signature with DP and signature with RTA fields for each ISIN, fund, or folio. Self-attested Address proof. Proof can be any one of the following: Voters ID, driving licence, passport, Aadhaar, bank statement. If you submit Aadhaar as proof, ensure you submit a masked Aadhaar. Self-attested PAN. 1 Annexure (DOC) for each mutual fund signed in the signature with DP and the signature with RTA fields. Submission process Create a ticket and attach a soft copy of all the documents. Your documents will be reviewed in case any corrections are required. After review, courier the documents to: Zerodha Customer Support Centre 192A 4th Floor, Kalyani Vista, 3rd Main Road, JP Nagar 4th Phase, Bengaluru, 560076 Processing time: Rematerialisation of mutual funds can take up to 25 working days. Charges for rematerialisation Type Charges (₹) + 18% GST Rematerialisation request per scheme ₹150 + ₹5.50 per transaction (CDSL charges) + 18% GST Rematerialisation request for ELSS funds per investment ₹150 + ₹5.50 per transaction (CDSL charges) + 18% GST Your rematerialisation request will not be processed if your Zerodha account does not have enough funds to cover the charges. You can check the complete tariff sheet for more information about charges. If an ELSS investment is made using SIPs, rematerialisation charges will be levied for every instalment. Assume 10 monthly SIPs are made into an ELSS fund, and a rematerialisation request is placed. Charges will be applicable for each individual SIP instalment. You can also rematerialise your equity shares by following the procedure for rematerialising shares.
title: How to rematerialise mutual funds?

text: You can transfer your mutual fund holdings out of Coin in three ways: Transfer to another CDSL demat account: The transfer can be done online via CDSL easiest. Transfer to an NSDL demat account: The transfer is done off-market. Transfer to a non-demat mode (Physical mode, RTA Mode or Offline Mode): The mutual fund should be rematerialised from the previous broker or distributor to transfer the units. Transferring units under lock-in period There are two ways to transfer mutual fund units which are under a lock-in period: Rematerialisation for NSDL account: Locked-in units cannot be transferred in demat mode. If the units are under the lock-in period, they have to be rematerialised and then transferred. Closure cum transfer for CDSL accounts: The transfer of units under lock-in can only be done to a CDSL demat account under the same PAN using the closure cum transfer process. If your employer doesn't allow trading or investing with Zerodha, you can get Zerodha empanelled as a registered broker with your employer Transfer charges The charges for rematerialisation are ₹ 150 + 18% GST. For ELSS funds, the rematerialisation charge is ₹ 150 per investment. See the tariff sheet (PDF) to know more. The first DIS booklet of 10 slips is free. For additional booklets, ₹100 + 18% GST is charged per booklet, along with courier charges of ₹100 + 18% GST. Transfer charges of ₹25 per security, per transaction, plus 18% GST, are applicable. For example, if you transfer 10 units of HDFC Balanced Advantage Fund, 5 units of SBI Blue Chip Fund and 2 units of Quant Small Cap Fund in one transaction, the charges would be ₹75 + 18% GST. If you transfer 10 shares of HDFC Balanced Advantage Fund in two transactions, the charges would be ₹50 + 18% GST.

text: Conditional orders are NAV-tracking orders that allow you to buy or sell mutual funds when they reach a specific NAV price you select, similar to how orders are triggered for stocks. You can use this feature only for lump sum investments, not for SIPs. With conditional orders, you can purchase or redeem mutual funds at an NAV that matches your investment strategy. For purchase orders, you will receive the NAV based on when BSE Clearing Limited (BSECL) receives your funds. For sell or redemption orders, the applicable NAV will be that of the day when the order is triggered and processed. How to place a conditional buy order? On Coin web Login to coin.zerodha.com. Search for the fund. Click on the fund and then on Buy. Select the payment method and enter the investment amount. Click on Conditional order and enter the NAV. Click on Buy now. Click on Confirm. On Coin app Search for the fund. Tap on Buy. Enter the Investment amount and tap on Conditional order. Enter the NAV and tap on Place conditional order. Tap on Invest. How to place a conditional redemption order? On Coin web Visit coin.zerodha.com/dashboard/mf/portfolio. Select the fund and click on the menu icon. Click on Redeem. Enter the number of units to redeem or click on Redeem all. Click on Conditional order and enter the NAV. Click on Place conditional order. Click on Confirm. Non-DDPI/POA clients will have to authorise the units at CDSL to complete the redemption. Enter the amount and your trigger price. Click on Place conditional order. Click on Confirm. If you are a non-DDPI/POA client, you will need to authorise your units at CDSL to complete the redemption. On Coin app Tap on Investments. Select the fund and tap on the menu icon. Tap on Redeem. Enter
title: What is a conditional order and how to place it on Coin?

text: you are a non-DDPI/POA client, you will need to authorise your units at CDSL to complete the redemption. On Coin app Tap on Investments. Select the fund and tap on the menu icon. Tap on Redeem. Enter the number of units to redeem or tap on Redeem all units. Tap on Conditional order. Enter the NAV and tap on Place conditional order. Tap on Redeem. If you are a non-DDPI/POA client, you will need to authorise your units at CDSL to complete the redemption. After BSE Clearing Limited (BSECL) processes your payout according to the scheme's settlement time, your funds will be credited to your primary bank account linked with Zerodha within 24 working hours, depending on your bank. The redemption time can be viewed on the order window. You can check your order status in the order history by visiting coin.zerodha.com/dashboard/mf/orders or through the orders section in the Coin app. Price limits for conditional orders Your trigger price must fall within specific ranges: Buy orders: You cannot set the trigger price below 60% of the current NAV Sell orders: You cannot set the trigger price above 200% of the current NAV Example scenarios Buy order: If a mutual fund's current NAV is ₹100, you cannot set your trigger price below ₹60 (60% of ₹100). If you set your trigger price at ₹70, your order will trigger when the NAV reaches or falls to ₹70. Sell order: If a mutual fund's current NAV is ₹100, you cannot set your trigger price above ₹200. If you set your trigger price at ₹195, your redemption order will trigger when the NAV reaches ₹195. Your conditional orders remain valid for 365 days and stay pending until triggered or until this period expires, after which they are automatically rejected.
title: What is a conditional order and how to place it on Coin?

text: You can easily manage your mutual fund watchlist on the Coin app by adding funds you want to monitor, removing funds you no longer need, and rearranging them in your preferred order. Adding mutual funds to your watchlist To add mutual funds to your watchlist: Tap on Discover and search for the funds. Tap on the fund you want to add. Tap on Add to watchlist. You can access your watchlist on the Coin app by tapping on Discover and then on Watchlist. Removing mutual funds from your watchlist To remove mutual funds from your watchlist: Tap on Your watchlist. Tap and hold on any fund to edit the watchlist. Tap on the Bin icon next to the fund you want to remove. Rearranging mutual funds on your watchlist To rearrange the order of mutual funds on your watchlist: Tap and hold on any fund to edit the watchlist. Tap on the left side of the fund, hold, and drag to move it to your preferred position. Tap on Save changes. Things to keep in mind You can add up to 50 mutual fund schemes to your watchlist. Your watchlist syncs automatically between the Coin app and Coin web. Any changes you make on one platform will appear on the other. The percentage change in Net Asset Value (NAV) of a scheme is calculated from the day you added the scheme to your watchlist. You can view the date you added the scheme and its Assets Under Management (AUM) on Coin web.
title: How to add, remove, or rearrange mutual funds from your watchlist on the Coin app?

text: Yes, the expense ratio already includes GST. You do not need to pay GST separately on mutual fund investments. Asset Management Companies (AMCs) provide money management services, which fall under GST regulations. The AMC incorporates this GST into the expense ratio, so you see the final cost that includes all applicable taxes. When you review a mutual fund's expense ratio, this percentage represents your total annual cost, including GST.
title: Is GST included in the mutual fund expense ratio?

Settlement timelines Scheme category Settlement to demat account Visible on Console Liquid T Day by 7 PM T+1 day Non-Liquid T+1 Day by 7 PM T+2 day The applicable NAV depends on when your funds are credited to ICCL's bank account. Did you know? When units are delivered late to the demat account, NA is displayed on T+2 days after the allotment of units only for one day and is rectified on the next day (T+3). There could be a late delivery of units to the demat account.

text: IDCW stands for Income Distribution Capital Withdrawal option, which is a dividend distribution plan for mutual funds. How IDCW works When you invest in an IDCW scheme, you receive dividends as partial payouts from the profits earned by the fund's underlying investments. After the fund distributes these dividends to you, the Net Asset Value (NAV) of the fund decreases proportionally. Important details about IDCW Dividend tracking: You can track your dividends on the respective Asset Management Company's (AMC) website. Reinvestment limitation: IDCW reinvestment schemes are currently not available on Coin.
title: What does IDCW on Coin mean?

text: You do not need to add a separate nominee for your mutual fund holdings on Coin. Your demat account nominee automatically applies to all mutual funds you purchase through Coin since these investments are held in demat form. You can add a nominee online for your Zerodha account. Did you know? If a nominee has already been added to the demat account, please ignore any communication from the Asset Management Company (AMC) about the nomination. Nominees can be checked by visiting console.zerodha.com/account/nominee.
title: How to add a nominee for mutual fund holdings on Coin?

text: Yes, you pay stamp duty at 0.005% on all mutual fund purchases through Coin. The stamp duty is automatically adjusted against your allotted units rather than charged separately. How stamp duty affects your investment When you invest in mutual funds, the stamp duty reduces your actual investment amount, which affects the number of units you receive. Example calculation Stamp duty calculation: ₹10,000 × 0.005% = ₹0.50 (50 paisa) Adjusted investment amount: ₹10,000 - ₹0.50 = ₹9,999.50 Units allotted: If the NAV is ₹10, you receive ₹9,999.50 ÷ ₹10 = 999.95 units Your investment summary shows the full ₹10,000 investment, but you receive units worth ₹9,999.50 after stamp duty adjustment. When purchasing mutual funds, stamp duty is currently not displayed under charges on Console.
title: Is stamp duty applicable for mutual fund purchases on Coin?

text: Covered bonds are debt securities that banks or Non-Banking Financial Companies (NBFCs) issue with backing from a pool of assets. These bonds ensure that if the issuer defaults, you can recover your investment from the collateralised asset pool. How covered bonds work When you invest in covered bonds, the structure provides you with enhanced security through this process: The bank or NBFC issues bonds to you and other investors. The issuer establishes a cover pool of secured loans as security. At maturity, the bank or NBFC repays your principal amount plus interest. If the issuer fails to make payments, you can recover your investment from the cover pool. The cover pool typically includes secured loans such as housing loans, vehicle loans, gold loans, and other asset-backed lending. Key advantages for investors Dual recourse protection: You receive two levels of security with covered bonds: Primary recourse against the issuer Secondary recourse against the bankruptcy-protected cover pool assets This differs from secured corporate bonds, which only provide recourse against the issuer. Higher credit ratings: Covered bonds generally receive higher credit ratings than the issuer's standalone rating due to the additional asset pool security. You cannot pledge covered bonds as collateral with Zerodha. To view the complete list of instruments you can pledge for margins and their applicable haircut percentages, visit zerodha.com/approved-securities.
title: What are covered bonds?

text: A Specialised Investment Fund (SIF) is a SEBI-regulated vehicle that blends the characteristics of mutual funds and Alternative Investment Funds (AIFs), designed for high-net-worth individuals (HNIs) and experienced investors with a minimum investment of ₹10 lakh. SIFs bridge the gap between traditional mutual funds and more exclusive products like Portfolio Management Services (PMS) and AIFs, giving fund managers greater flexibility to use a wider range of market strategies within SEBI's regulatory framework. SEBI introduced SIFs in late 2024, formally released the regulatory framework for SIFs in February 2025, and made it effective from April 2025, enabling AMCs to launch India's first SIF offerings. How to invest in SIFs on Coin You can invest in SIFs directly through the Coin app or Coin web. Coin currently offers SIF investments in open-ended Growth Schemes. Upcoming open-ended SIF NFOs will also be available on Coin during the NFO period. AMC SIP: The minimum SIP instalment is ₹10 lakh. AMC SIPs below ₹10 lakh are not permitted. Coin SIP: Complete a lumpsum initial investment of ₹10 lakh in the SIF scheme and receive unit allotment. Once your initial investment is allotted, you can set up a Coin SIP at lower instalment amounts. The minimum additional purchase amount is determined by the respective AMC and may vary across schemes. SIF structures SIFs are available in two structures: Interval SIFs: Mandatorily listed on stock exchanges. You can buy or redeem units only during predefined intervals set by the fund house, typically twice a week. The fund house announces the specific days in the Scheme Information Document (SID) during the New Fund Offer (NFO) period. Open-ended SIFs: Not required to be listed on exchanges. You can directly purchase or redeem units through the fund house on an ongoing basis, similar to regular mutual funds. Redemption conditions and
title: What are Specialised Investment Funds (SIFs) and how to invest in them?

text: New Fund Offer (NFO) period. Open-ended SIFs: Not required to be listed on exchanges. You can directly purchase or redeem units through the fund house on an ongoing basis, similar to regular mutual funds. Redemption conditions and unit freezing If your investment value falls below ₹10 lakh due to partial redemptions, SEBI requires your remaining units to be frozen. Once frozen, you cannot redeem, transfer, or switch your units until the value is restored. You have 30 days to make an additional investment in the same scheme and bring your value back to at least ₹10 lakh. If you do not restore the value within 30 days, the AMC will automatically redeem all your remaining units and transfer the proceeds to your registered bank account. This condition applies to non-accredited investors only. title: What are Specialised Investment Funds (SIFs) and how to invest in them?

text: Equity-Linked Savings Schemes (ELSS) are diversified equity mutual fund schemes offered by Asset Management Companies (AMCs) in India that provide tax deductions under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961 while helping you build long-term wealth. You can invest in these funds through Systematic Investment Plans (SIP) or lump sum investments. Key features Lock-in period: Your investments have a 3-year lock-in period from the date of purchase, during which you cannot redeem or transfer your units. Tax benefits: You can claim tax deductions of up to ₹1,50,000 during the lock-in period. Taxation on profits: Depending on the holding period, your profits are subject to Long-Term Capital Gains (LTCG) or Short-Term Capital Gains (STCG) tax. How to download ELSS statement from Coin To download your ELSS statement from Coin: Login to coin.zerodha.com. Click on Mutual funds. Click on ELSS statement and select the financial year. Your ELSS mutual fund units will appear in the statement after they settle in your demat account on T+2 day. How to download ELSS statement from Console Alternatively, you can download your ELSS statement from Console: Visit console.zerodha.com. Click on Reports. Click on Downloads. From the drop-down menu, select ELSS statement, choose the financial year and click on Download.
title: What are ELSS funds, and how can I download the ELSS statement?

text: An AMC SIP (Asset Management Company Systematic Investment Plan) is a systematic investment plan that you set up directly with the fund house through Coin as your interface. Unlike regular SIPs managed by Zerodha, AMC SIPs are registered and maintained directly by the Asset Management Company (AMC). How to set up AMC SIPs on Coin app To set up an AMC SIP on the Coin app: Select your desired fund and then on SIP . Tap on the checkbox for AMC SIP . Select a Mandate (optional). Enter your Instalment amount and enter your preferred Frequency and Date . Tap on Create AMC SIP and then tap on Create . How to set up AMC SIPs on Coin web To set up an AMC SIP on Coin web: Select a Mandate (optional). Search for your desired fund and click on SIP. Click on the checkbox for AMC SIP. Enter your Instalment Amount. Enter your preferred Frequency and Date. Click on Create SIP and then click on Confirm. Things to keep in mind You must set up your Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) at least 2 days before your preferred execution date to ensure it starts within the current month. For example, if you want your SIP to execute on the 5th of each month, you need to set it up on or before the 2nd of the current month. If you set up your SIP on the 3rd with an execution date of the 5th, it will start from the next month. BSE sends you an email to your registered email address confirming successful registration and providing your SIP registration number when you create an AMC SIP. The applicable NAV depends on when your funds are credited to BSECL's bank account. You can complete your SIP payments from the Pending payments
title: What is an AMC SIP and how to create one on Coin?

text: registration and providing your SIP registration number when you create an AMC SIP. The applicable NAV depends on when your funds are credited to BSECL's bank account. You can complete your SIP payments from the Pending payments section if you haven't linked a mandate to your SIP. Your AMC determines the minimum investment amount for AMC SIPs. You cannot modify, pause, or step up an AMC SIP since the system creates AMC SIPs directly with the AMC rather than internally with your account. You must delete or cancel your AMC SIPs at least 2 days before the next instalment date.
title: What is an AMC SIP and how to create one on Coin?

text: As per the circular (PDF), starting April 1, 2024, AMCs will cancel your SIP if payment for three consecutive instalments fails, effective from April 1, 2024. If your SIP gets cancelled due to payment failures, you need to create a new AMC SIP to continue investing. AMCs may also decide to cancel the SIPs for these reasons: Merger of schemes. Suspension or restrictions on schemes. Consolidation of schemes. Winding up of schemes. Zerodha SIPs will not be cancelled if payment for three consecutive SIPs fails. However, they will be cancelled if the scheme has undergone any corporate action, like a merger, or if it has been suspended.
title: Why were the AMC SIPs cancelled?

text: You can calculate your Return on Investments (ROI) using Zerodha's SIP calculator for lump sum or SIPs in different ways, depending on your investment type and duration. Lump sum investment for less than a year Method: Absolute Return When you make a lump sum investment for less than a year, use absolute return to get a true picture of your earnings. Let’s say you made a lump sum investment of Rs. 2,75,000 on 28 May 2018 and the present value of the investment is Rs. 325000. In such a case, calculating the absolute return will give a true picture of your earnings. Example: You invested ₹2,75,000 on 28 May 2018, and your investment is now worth ₹3,25,000 on 10 December 2018. Date Amount 2018/05/28 -275000 2018/12/10 325000 Absolute Return 18.18% *Negative Values indicate cash outflows. Absolute Return % = (Current Value - Investment Value) / Investment Value x 100 = (325000 - 275000) / 275000 * 100 = 18.18 Absolute return shows your investment growth without considering the time period. It simply calculates the percentage difference between your initial investment and current value. Lump sum investment over multiple years Method: CAGR (Compounded Annual Growth Rate) When you make a lump sum investment for more than a year, CAGR provides the right picture of your returns. CAGR represents the consistent rate at which your investment would have grown if it had compounded at the same rate each year. Example: You invested ₹2,75,000 on 23 May 2018, and your investment is worth ₹3,25,000 on 23 July 2019. Date Amount 2018/05/23 -275000 2019/07/23 325000 Duration 425 days CAGR 15.43% *Negative Values indicate cash outflows. CAGR = [(Current Value / Investment Value)^(365/Number of Days)] - 1 = [(325000/275000)^(365/425)] - 1 = 0.1543 [15.43%] Case 3: SIP for less than a year SIPs Method: XIRR
title: How to calculate mutual fund returns?

text: days CAGR 15.43% *Negative Values indicate cash outflows. CAGR = [(Current Value / Investment Value)^(365/Number of Days)] - 1 = [(325000/275000)^(365/425)] - 1 = 0.1543 [15.43%] Case 3: SIP for less than a year SIPs Method: XIRR (Extended Internal Rate of Return) When you invest through SIP for less than a year, calculate absolute profit for your investments. Absolute profit to be considered for investments less than a year. When your SIP runs for more than one year, use XIRR to calculate returns accurately. Example: You started a monthly SIP of ₹10,000 from December 2017 to January 2019, and your portfolio is worth ₹1,80,000. Date Amount 2017/12/05 -10000 2018/01/05 -10000 2018/02/05 -10000 2018/03/05 -10000 2018/04/05 -10000 2018/05/05 -10000 2018/06/05 -10000 2018/07/05 -10000 2018/08/05 -10000 2018/09/05 -10000 2018/10/05 -10000 2018/11/05 -10000 2018/12/05 -10000 2019/01/05 -10000 2019/01/07 180000 XIRR 54.96% *Negative Values indicate cash outflows. You can read more about mutual fund return calculations by visiting Measuring Mutual fund Returns module on Varsity. For a video walkthrough on XIRR, see How to check XIRR for individual investments and entire portfolio?
title: How to calculate mutual fund returns?

text: You can delete a mutual fund SIP by following these steps: Log in to Coin. Click on Mutual funds. Click on SIPs Click on the fund. Click on Delete. Did you know? An AMC SIP can be deleted 2 days prior to the SIP trigger date.

text: To pause or resume SIPs, follow these steps: Click on the drop-down and select Mutual funds. Click on SIPs. Select the mutual fund scheme. Click on Pause to pause a SIP or Resume to resume the SIP. Click on Yes. Did you know? AMC SIPs cannot be paused. To learn more, see What is the AMC SIP option on Coin web? Zerodha SIP orders should be paused or resumed before the cut-off time, i.e., T-1 day before 5:30 PM for it to be effective for the current month. To ensure that a Systematic Investment Plan (SIP) starts within the current month, it must be set up at least 2 days prior to the preferred execution date. For example, if the client wants the SIP to execute on the 5th of each month, they need to set it up on or before the 2nd of the current month. If the SIP is set up on the 3rd with an execution date of the 5th, it will start from the next month.
title: How can SIPs be paused and resumed on Coin web?

text: You can watch this video in Hindi here. Microsavings on Coin allows you to invest small amounts daily through Micro SIPs. You can invest starting from ₹10 onwards every day, making it easier to build investment discipline with minimal amounts.Currently, you can use microsavings to invest in the Zerodha Nifty LargeMidcap 250 Index Fund, Zerodha Multi Asset Passive FoF, and Zerodha Gold ETF FoF. To start microsavings, you need to create a UPI autopay mandate. How to create a microsavings SIP Coin app Tap on Microsavings. Tap on the fund's name and tap on Create Micro SIP. Select your mandate. If you have not created any mandates, tap on Manage mandates, then tap on New mandate to create one. Enter the amount and tap on Create Micro SIP. Tap on Confirm. Coin web Log in to Coin. Click on Invest now beside Microsavings. Click on Invest. Select your mandate. If you have not created any mandates, click on Manage mandates, then click on New mandate to create one. Enter the amount and click on Create. Click on Confirm. Your first SIP instalment will be executed from T+2 days onwards. The UPI autopay debit is triggered on T+1 day morning for the T+2 day execution. For example, if you create your SIP on Monday, the UPI autopay debit will be triggered on Tuesday morning, and the first instalment will be executed on Wednesday. Managing your microsavings SIPs You can track, modify, or delete your micro SIPs from the SIP section on Coin.

text: A step-up SIP automatically increases your SIP amount by a predefined percentage each year. This feature helps scale your investments alongside your income. For example, if you start a SIP of ₹10,000 with a 10% step-up, your SIP amount will increase to ₹11,000 (10% of ₹10,000) in the second year and ₹12,100 (10% of ₹11,000) in the third year. Calculate your estimated returns based on your planned step-up percentage using Zerodha's step-up SIP calculator. How to create a new step-up SIP on Coin app Select the mutual fund and tap on SIP. Select a mandate (Optional). Tap on the check box for Automatic step-up. Enter the Increment percentage to select the percentage by which your SIP should increase. Select Month and Day. Tap on Create SIP. Tap on Create. How to enable step-up on existing SIPs on Coin app Tap on Investments and then on SIPs. Tap on the fund and then on Modify. Enter the Increment percentage to select the percentage by which your SIP should increase. Select Month and Day. Tap on Modify SIP. How to create a new step-up SIP on Coin web Log in to Coin. Select the mutual fund and click on SIP. Select a mandate (Optional). Click on the check box for Automatic step-up. Click on Increment percentage to select the percentage by which your SIP should increase. Select the date under Choose date. Click on Create SIP. How to enable step-up on existing SIPs on Coin web Select the mutual fund scheme and click on Modify. Click on the check box for Automatic step-up. Click on Increment percentage to select the percentage by which the SIP should increase. Select the date from Choose date. Click on Update SIP and then on Confirm. Things to keep in mind The step-up percentage multiple varies from
